title: Relentless Rage
description: Your Rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 Hit Points while your Rage is active and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Barbarian level.\n\nEach time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a Short or Long Rest, the DC resets to 10.

title: Exhaustion
description:
### Exhaustion

Some special abilities and environmental hazards, such as starvation and the long-term effects of freezing or scorching temperatures, can lead to a special condition called exhaustion. Exhaustion is measured in six levels. An effect can give a creature one or more levels of exhaustion, as specified in the effect's description.

1.    Disadvantage on ability checks

2.    Speed halved

3.    Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws

4.    Hit point maximum halved

5.    Speed reduced to 0

6.    Death

title: Persistent Rage
description: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended uses of Rage. After you regain uses of Rage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.\n\nIn addition, your Rage is so fierce that is now lasts for 10 minutes without you needing to do anything to extend it from round to round. Your Rage ends early if you have the Unconscious condition (not just the Incapacitated condition) or don Heavy armor.

title: Petrified
description:
### Petrified

- A petrified creature is transformed, along with any nonmagical object it is wearing or carrying, into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone). Its weight increases by a factor of ten, and it ceases aging.
- The creature is incapacitated (see the condition), can't move or speak, and is unaware of its surroundings.
- Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.
- The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
- The creature has resistance to all damage.
- The creature is immune to poison and disease, although a poison or disease already in its system is suspended, not neutralized.

title: Strength Modifier
description: How a character's strength influences their skills.

title: Initiative
description: Influences your turn order in combat

title: Wisdom Modifier
description: How a character's wisdom influences their skills.

title: Reckless Attack
description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with increased ferocity. When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.

title: Hit Points
description: A measurement of well being.

title: Unconscious
description:
### Unconscious

- An unconscious creature is incapacitated, can't move or speak, and is unaware of its surroundings.
- The creature drops whatever it's holding and falls prone.
- The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
- Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.
- Any attack that hits the creature is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of the creature.

title: Indomitable Might
description: If your total for a Strength check or Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

title: Self-Restoration
description: Through sheer force of will, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned.\n\nIn addition, forgoing food and drink doesn't give you levels of Exhaustion.

title: Danger Sense
description: You gain an uncanny sense of when things aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge perils. You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

title: Restrained
description:
### Restrained

- A restrained creature's speed becomes 0, and it can't benefit from any bonus to its speed.
- Attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and the creature's attack rolls have disadvantage.
- The creature has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

title: Backstory
description: Notes about the character's backstory

title: Indomitable
description: If you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Fighter level. You must use the new roll, and you can't use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest.\n\nYou can use this feature twice before a Long Rest starting at level 13 and three times before a Long Rest starting at level 17.

title: Level
description: A measurement of how powerful or skilled a character is.

title: Race
description: A character's ancestry and biological background.

title: Spell Save DC
description: How difficult it is for targets to resist your spells.

title: Unreactive
description: Unable to take reactions

title: Deafened
description: ### Deafened\n\n- A deafened creature can't hear and automatically fails any ability check that requires hearing.

title: Armor Class
description: How difficult it is to hit you with an attack.

title: Class
description: A character's archetype.

title: Blinded
description:
### Blinded

- A blinded creature can't see and automatically fails any ability check that requires sight.
- Attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and the creature's attack rolls have disadvantage.

title: Peerless Skill
description: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration; roll the Bardic Inspiration die, and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning a failure into a success. On a failure, the Bardic Inspiration isn't expended.

title: Tactical Mind
description: You have a mind for tactics on and off the battlefield. When you fail an ability check, you can expend a use of your Second Wind to push yourself toward success. Rather than regaining Hit Points, you roll 1d10 and add the number rolled to the ability check, potentially turning it into a success. If the check still fails, this use of Second Wind ins't expended.

title: Speed
description: How far you may move during a single turn of combat.

title: Proficiency Bonus
description: A modifier to add to checks for skills in which you are proficient.

title: Inspiration
description: Points to be used at the Game Master's discretion.

title: Hit Dice
description: The type of die used to calculate your hit points.

title: Grappled
description:
### Grappled

- A grappled creature's speed becomes 0, and it can't benefit from any bonus to its speed.
- The condition ends if the grappler is incapacitated (see the condition).
- The condition also ends if an effect removes the grappled creature from the reach of the grappler or grappling effect, such as when a creature is hurled away by the Thunderwave spell.

title: Unarmored Defense
description: While you aren't wearing any armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Constitution modifiers. You can use a Shield and still gain this benefit.

title: Charisma
description: A character's charm and social influence.

title: Prone
description:
### Prone

- A prone creature's only movement option is to crawl, unless it stands up and thereby ends the condition.
- The creature has disadvantage on attack rolls.
- An attack roll against the creature has advantage if the attacker is within 5 feet of the creature. Otherwise, the attack roll has disadvantage.

title: Strength
description: A character's physical strength.

title: Paralyzed
description:
### Paralyzed

- A paralyzed creature is incapacitated (see the condition) and can't move or speak.
- The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
- Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.
- Any attack that hits the creature is a critical hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of the creature.

title: Temporary Hit Points
description: Additional hit points from an outside source. You may only have one source of temporary hit points at a time.

title: Intelligence Modifier
description: How a character's intelligence influences their skills.

title: Frightened
description:
### Frightened

- A frightened creature has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of its fear is within line of sight.
- The creature can't willingly move closer to the source of its fear.

title: Uncanny Metabolism
description: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, roll your Martial Arts die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Monk level plus the number rolled.\n\nOnce you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Constitution Modifier
description: How a character's constitution influences their skills.

title: Spell Casting Ability
description: Which ability score influences a character's ability to cast spells.

title: Invisible
description:
### Invisible

- An invisible creature is impossible to see without the aid of magic or a special sense. For the purpose of hiding, the creature is heavily obscured. The creature's location can be detected by any noise it makes or any tracks it leaves.
- Attack rolls against the creature have disadvantage, and the creature's attack rolls have advantage.

title: Intelligence
description: How quickly a character grasps complex concepts.

title: Feral Instinct
description: Your instincts are so honed that you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

title: Spell Attack Modifier
description: The modifier added to spell attacks to determine if they hit their target.

title: Charmed
description:
### Charmed

- A charmed creature can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with harmful abilities or magical effects.
- The charmer has advantage on any ability check to interact socially with the creature.

title: Hit Point Maximum
description: The maximum number of hit points you may have.

title: Alignment
description: A character's general motivation for decision making.
options: Chaotic Evil|Neutral Evil|Lawful Evil|Chaotic Neutral|True Neutral|Lawful Neutral|Chaotic Good|Neutral Good|Lawful Good

title: Stunned
description:
### Stunned

- A stunned creature is incapacitated (see the condition), can't move, and can speak only falteringly.
- The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.

title: Passive Perception
description: How easily you notice things without prior awareness.

title: Wisdom
description: A character's presence of mind.

title: Constitution
description: A character's will and ability to resist physical ailment.

title: Dexterity
description: How gracefully a character moves.

title: Experience Points
description: Determines when to advance to the next level.

title: Charisma Modifier
description: How a character's charisma influences their skills.

title: Poisoned
description: ### Poisoned\n\n- A poisoned creature has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

title: Cutting Words
description: You learn to use your wit to supernaturally distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself makes a damage roll or succeeds on an ability check or attack roll, you can take a Reaction to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration; roll your Bardic Inspiration die and subtract the number rolled from the creature's roll, reducing the damage or potentially turning the success into a failure.

title: Dexterity Modifier
description: How a character's dexterity influences their skills.

title: Incapacitated
description: ### Incapacitated\n\n- An incapacitated creature can't take actions or reactions.
